items.title: Yoshi and the Mysterious Book releases on Switch 2 tomorrow, score the pre-order deal while you still can
items.summary:
The new Nintendo Switch 2: Choose Your Game Bundle is still available for purchase for what might very well be the very best deal we see all year on Switch 2 consoles – this is a limited run so get in there if you’re looking to grab a console still. But with the new brand new Yoshi and the Mysterious Book set for release tomorrow, we wanted to make sure the ongoing pre-order offer was front and center for folks who already have one. Physical copies of this one for your collection carry a $70 list price, but you can still score a copy at $59.88 via Amazon right now. And, much like the switch 2 console, there’s really no telling how long I will take to see a better deal on this one. Reviews are largely positive across the board thus far on Yoshi’s new adventure and if you didn’t secure a copy yet, now’s your chance. 

  • Yoshi and the Mysterious Book $60 (Reg. $70)
  • Star Fox Switch 2 $50 (Reg. $60) | Amazon
  • Star Fox Switch 2 $50 (Reg. $60) | Walmart
  • Splatoon Raiders $50 (Reg. $60)

New Nintendo Switch 2: Choose Your Game console bundle deal now available for purchase

items.title: Don’t miss Acer’s 16-inch Nitro V RTX 5070 gaming laptop while it’s down to $1,200 and comes with FREE 007 First Light game code
items.summary:
We just spotted a deal at B&H that drops an Acer Nitro V RTX 5070 gaming laptop down to $1,199.99 shipped. This particular config with an Intel Core 7 240H processor and 16GB of RAM typically sells for around $1,400, and is currently listed for $1,470 directly through Acer’s online store. B&H’s offer cuts $270 off that price, leaving you with sizable savings you can put toward accessories or peripherals for your setup. You are looking at some serious value here with this deal, considering similarly-specced gaming laptops with an RTX 5070 often creep well beyond the $1,600 mark. In fact, Acer is currently listing a version of the same Nitro V equipped with the lower-tier RTX 5050 for $1,670, which makes this B&H deal worth looking into. Notably, the listing also includes a FREE 007 First Light game code worth $70, stacking even more value on top of the already substantial discount. Head below for more details.

more…

items.title: Lenovo’s budget-friendly Android Idea Tab with stylus is down to $152 (All-time low)
items.summary:
As part of its ongoing Memorial Day sale, Lenovo is offering its Idea Tab Android tablet bundled with a stylus for $151.99 shipped. Be sure to enter promo code EXTRAFIVE at checkout to see the discounted price. This budget-friendly model usually sells for around $240, which means you’re saving roughly $88 with today’s deal. That works out to about a 36% discount, which also marks the lowest price we’ve tracked so far for the 8GB RAM + 128GB storage configuration paired with the stylus. While we’ve seen frequent discounts on the lower-end 4GB model and even Amazon bundles that include the tablet, stylus, and folio case, this is the best price we’ve spotted yet for the standalone tablet with the stylus. Head below for more details, along with additional deals on other Lenovo tablets.

items.title: Juiced Bikes is back with its moto-style Scrambler hardtail + full suspension e-bikes and $245 FREE bundles from $1,699
items.summary:
One of the staples of America’s e-bike market, Juiced Bikes, is officially back from the dead, thanks to Lectric’s 11th-hour save during 2024’s financial turmoil, and it’s kicking off its rebirth with the launch of two new e-bikes! You can now score the Scrambler Moto-Style Hardtail e-bike at $1,699 shipped, while the Scrambler Moto-Style Full Suspension e-bike sits a little higher at $1,899 shipped – and both models come with a $245 bundle of FREE gear. These new rides seem to be following the usual Lectric playbook of offering discounts on the bundled gear rather than the e-bikes themselves, with these bundles including a personalized nameplate, a headplate, a bar bag, a bar pad, a fender set, and a sticker pack.

more…

Amazon’s Memorial Day Sale is officially here, and it’s packed with deals on all the backyard, cookout, and hosting essentials you’ll want for summer. Whether you’re planning a long weekend BBQ, upgrading the patio setup, or just looking for an excuse to finally grab that big-ticket outdoor item sitting in your cart, now’s the time to shop. Hundreds of outdoor gear and entertaining favorites are seeing up to 40% off, including smokeless fire pits, patio lighting, bug-repelling gadgets, gardening tools, outdoor furniture, tailgating gear, and more.

items.title: Lectric launches new XPress2 cruiser + commuter e-bikes with FREE $336 gear bundle and loads of upgrades at $1,399
items.summary:
Following the surprise first-time discount on Aventon’s Current ADV eMTB, Lectric is now launching its newest second-generation XPress2 Long-Range Cruiser e-bike with a $336 FREE bundle of gear for $1,399 shipped, while the High-Step model is getting the moniker of a commuter e-bike. As you probably already know, Lectric very rarely cuts the actual prices on its e-bikes and instead discounts the bundled add-on gear, which would have this bundle package at $1,735 if that were not the case. It’s the average bundle size we normally see on the first-generation XPress e-bikes, containing a rear cargo rack, fender set, Elite headlight, and a suspension seatpost. We’re no longer seeing the Step-Through XPress1 on the website, so it’s safe to assume that once stock runs out on the high-step model, they will be fully replaced by this newer variation – and it’s only sitting $100 higher in price. Head below to learn more about it.

more…

items.title: Tested: This Hall effect aluminum unibody keyboard has bold looks backed by some serious gaming chops
items.summary:
In a world where most keyboards have started looking increasingly similar, all following more or less the same design language, it always catches my attention when something with a more aggressive gaming aesthetic – sharp angles, bold cuts, and a bit more personality – shows up every now and then. The IQUNIX EV63 Hall effect keyboard we’re checking out today is one of those, currently sitting on my desk. Not only does it look good but it also checks a lot of the right boxes when it comes to gaming. I’ve spent enough time using it to get a solid feel for what it brings to the table, so head below for a closer look and to learn more about my experience with it.
